user_query:
I'm tracking wear trends on our robotic joint prototypes and need a simple local trend detector built from our cycling test summaries.

Inputs:
- CSV files under input/test_runs/ named run_*.csv. Each has columns: date,cycle_k,joint_id,peak_torque_nm,backlash_deg,temperature_c. Please do not hardcode filenames; inspect the input/test_runs directory and read all run_*.csv files you find.

What to do:
1) Load and combine all run_*.csv files, group by joint_id, and order each group by cycle_k (thousands of cycles). Only evaluate joints with at least 3 data points.
2) For each joint, fit two ordinary least squares regressions using cycle_k as the independent variable:
   - peak_torque_nm vs cycle_k
   - backlash_deg vs cycle_k
   Compute, for each metric, the slope (units per 1k cycles), R^2, start value (at smallest cycle_k), end value (at largest cycle_k), and delta (end − start). Use all available runs for each joint.
3) Flag trends using these criteria:
   - peak_torque_nm: flag if slope ≥ 0.0009 N·m per 1k cycles AND R^2 ≥ 0.9 (increasing only)
   - backlash_deg: flag if |slope| ≥ 0.00012 deg per 1k cycles AND R^2 ≥ 0.8 (report direction as increasing or decreasing)

Deliverables:
- output/metrics/per_joint_trends.csv: one row per joint_id with columns exactly: joint_id, n_points, cycle_k_min, cycle_k_max, torque_slope_nm_per_1k, torque_r2, torque_start_nm, torque_end_nm, torque_delta_nm, backlash_slope_deg_per_1k, backlash_r2, backlash_start_deg, backlash_end_deg, backlash_delta_deg.
- output/metrics/flags.json: a JSON array where each element has: joint_id, metric ("peak_torque_nm" or "backlash_deg"), direction ("increasing" or "decreasing"), slope_per_1k, r2, start_value, end_value, n_points, run_span ("<cycle_k_min>-<cycle_k_max>k"). Include only entries that meet the flagging criteria above.
- output/drafts/trend_email.txt: a short, plain-text email draft addressed to quality@example.com and tribology@example.com with subject "Robotic joint cycling trend check". In the body, summarize the flagged items as bullet points (one per flag with joint_id, metric, direction, slope per 1k, R^2, and end value), and include the relative paths to output/metrics/per_joint_trends.csv and output/metrics/flags.json. If no trends are flagged, state that explicitly and still reference the output files.

Notes:
- Treat directory inspection as essential: discover the input files by scanning input/test_runs/ for run_*.csv.
- All outputs must be written to the specified paths. The flags in output/metrics/flags.json should correspond to the rows in output/metrics/per_joint_trends.csv that meet the thresholds above.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
